step_by_step_thinking:
Which of the following is used to denote a continuous variable?
(A) Simple bar
(B) Histogram
(C) Pie diagram
(D) Multiple bar
Clinical Reasoning:
Continuous variables are often represented by graphical distributions like histograms, which display the frequency density across intervals. Among the choices, Option B, Histogram, aligns well with denoting continuous variables since it uses bars to illustrate varying densities of occurrences throughout specified intervals.
\boxed{B} Based on the context of continuously measured data, option B, Histogram, fits perfectly as it effectively captures the spread and concentration of measurements in successive intervals, thereby depicting continuous variables appropriately.
